label: Pre-Nursing Foundational Blueprint
description: Blueprint for pre-nursing and foundational learning content on NurseNest. Covers 12 focus areas across 92 topics intended to prepare students for entry-level nursing coursework and early-semester success. Sequencing follows a build-up model: terminology and basic science before anatomy, anatomy before pathophysiology, all sciences before pharmacology and assessment.
targetLearner: Pre-nursing student or first-semester nursing student requiring foundational science and clinical reasoning scaffolding

readinessWeightEnum.critical: Must-know before nursing school entry; high downstream dependency; failure here creates early-semester bottleneck
readinessWeightEnum.high: Very important for readiness; strongly tested in semester 1; missing this creates friction
readinessWeightEnum.medium: Important for full understanding but not acutely gating; gaps are recoverable
readinessWeightEnum.supporting: Enrichment or orientation context; helpful scaffolding but not a direct readiness blocker

cognitiveLevelEnum.foundational-recall: Primarily identification, naming, vocabulary, and classification at the pre-nursing level
cognitiveLevelEnum.conceptual-understanding: Explaining mechanisms, relationships between concepts, and how systems work — deeper than recall but not yet clinical decision-making
cognitiveLevelEnum.early-application: Using knowledge to perform a task, solve a problem, or make a basic decision in a nursing-adjacent scenario
